design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore castle rooms, break soft blocks with bubbles to find secrets, defeat bosses to gain power crystals and progress upward through four-story castle
design_philosophy.primary_tension: One-life permadeath system combined with hidden-mandatory secrets requiring trial-and-error or external knowledge; no visual differentiation between destructible soft blocks and solid hard blocks creates constant uncertainty
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Boy with tone deafness embarks on adventure to rescue Queen Eliza, defeat evil wizard Maharito, and reclaim stolen musical instruments from Hudson land
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately obscure secret-finding mechanic where progression depends on destroying indistinguishable blocks without clear guidance; rooms accessible in non-linear order but each contains only one fixed exit; unorthodox room design that doesn't follow left-to-right platformer flow; no invincibility frames after damage, making combat highly punishing

difficulty.lives_system: One life per game; player must start over completely upon death
difficulty.continues_system: Continue available by holding left on control pad + Start button after obtaining first crystal ball; allows return to progress point
difficulty.checkpointing: No traditional save points; continue feature serves as implicit checkpoint after first boss
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Low agency; difficulty is largely non-negotiable. Game is noted as 'extremely difficult and frustrating' due to mandatory secret-finding, lack of invincibility frames, and one-life system. No difficulty settings documented. Hidden items and shops scattered across world require luck or external knowledge to locate.

progression.upgrade_system: Boss-dropped crystals that upgrade Milon's bubble weapon (larger size, increased range); items from hidden shops augment specific attributes; honeycomb restores and extends health bar
progression.unlock_mechanism: Each castle story (floor) only accessible after defeating the boss of the previous story; seven total bosses (six Demon Beasts + Maharito) serve as progression gates
replayability_hooks: Music box minigame side quest that progressively adds new elf musicians with different instruments; replay encourages attempting to collect all music boxes for elaborate final song
Non-linear room order means multiple valid paths through castle on repeat playthroughs
Hidden shops and items scattered throughout encourage exploration on subsequent attempts

world_building_techniques: English game manual provides plot context (Milon's tone deafness disorder, Queen Eliza, Maharito as 'Warlord of the northern region')
Japanese title 'Meikyū Kumikyoku: Milon no Daibōken' (The Maze Suite: Milon's Great Adventure) emphasizes musical/adventure themes
Musical instrument collection quest reinforces world's tone-based communication culture
Boss names (Homa, Doma, Blue Doma, Red Homa, Balkarma, Kama) suggest hierarchical demon taxonomy

boss_design.philosophy: Seven hierarchical encounters serving as progression gates; each boss defeat grants crystal power-up and unlocks next castle story. Bosses framed as 'Demon Beasts' (魔獣) subordinate to final antagonist Maharito. Boss encounters require finding hidden paths to reach them—discovery itself is primary challenge.

faction_indicators: Maharito's demonic army vs. Hudson land inhabitants (implicit good/evil binary)
Queen Eliza as authority figure requiring rescue
Musical instrument ownership as cultural status (stolen by invaders)
danger_signals: Invading wizard Maharito and demonic forces occupying castle
Lightning bolt environment hazard that strikes if player stays outside castle too long (forces progression, punishes hesitation)
Instant death on single hit without invincibility frames (high lethality environment)
environmental_storytelling: Four-story castle vertical layout symbolizes progress toward confrontation with occupying forces
Hidden shops and secret rooms scattered throughout suggest civilian resistance/aid network
Multiple towers and well structure creates sense of massive, labyrinthine fortress requiring navigation skill
Musical theme reinforced through Maze Song minigame featuring progressive elf musicians building elaborate composition

sources_used: Wikipedia - Milon's Secret Castle (plot, gameplay mechanics, difficulty, progression, boss structure, release info)
